betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Machine Interface: Bridge to AE2 and External Automation§f

§aThe Machine Interface is the bridge between NC machines and AE2 networks.§f
The §bMachine Interface§f (`nuclearcraft:machine_interface`) is a §bspecial automation block§f in NuclearCraft. It exposes the internal state of an adjacent NC machine to external automation systems — primarily §bAE2 (Applied Energistics 2)§f networks — allowing storage buses, import/export buses, and §bCrafting Storage§f to interact with NC machines as if they were §bAE2-compatible inventories§f.

§6Crafting Recipe (graph: minecraft.crafting)§f:
Per the JEI graph, the Machine Interface recipe requires:
- §bLinear Actuator§f (`nuclearcraft:part:9`)
- §bElectric Motor§f (`nuclearcraft:part:8`)
- §bMachine Chassis§f (`nuclearcraft:part:10`)
- §bServomechanism§f (`nuclearcraft:part:7`)

§6Function§f:
- §bPlace the Machine Interface§f on any face of an NC machine (or pipe into it from an external automation system).
- §bThe Interface exposes§f the machine's §binput slots§f, §boutput slots§f, §bfluid tanks§f, and §benergy buffer§f to external automation.
- §bAE2 Storage Bus§f + §bMachine Interface§f: any item in an adjacent ME storage network will be §bvisible to the NC machine§f as if it were its own input; the machine's outputs will be §bvisible to the ME network§f as if they were on the bus.
- §bAE2 Import/Export Bus§f: works similarly for fluid and item transfer without filling the machine's GUI.

§6Direct Descendants (graph-confirmed)§f:
- §bDecay Neutron Collector§f (`nuclearcraft:decay_neutron_collector`, via NC Assembler)
- §bMachine Controller§f (`modularmachinery:blockcontroller`, used in Modular Machinery multiblocks)

§6Why It's Useful§f:
- §bAE2 integration§f: connect §bME Storage§f directly to §bNC machines§f without manual item transport.
- §bModular Machinery§f: the Machine Interface is a component in some §bModular Machinery§f multiblock controllers.
- §bDecay Neutron Collector§f: an NC machine that needs the Machine Interface as a chassis component.

§6Component Supply Chain§f:
- §bLinear Actuator§f → from §bSteel Ingot§f + §bIron Ingot§f in vanilla crafting
- §bElectric Motor§f → from §bCopper Coil§f + §bRedstone§f + §bIron Ingot§f in vanilla crafting
- §bMachine Chassis§f → from §bSteel Frame§f + §bTough Alloy Ingot§f in NC Manufactory
- §bServomechanism§f → from §bIron Ingot§f + §bRedstone§f + §bSteel Frame§f in vanilla crafting

§eTip§f: Place a §bMachine Interface§f on every NC machine that processes high-throughput recipes (Assemblers, Manufactories, Chemical Reactors) and connect it to your §bME Storage Network§f via §bStorage Bus§f — this eliminates manual item transport for the entire NC processing chain.
